questions.question: Explain the LIFO principle of stack with a real-life example.
keyPoints: LIFO stands for Last In First Out
Real-life example: stack of plates in cafeteria, or pile of books
The last plate/book added is the first one removed
Other examples: browser history, undo mechanism

questions.question: Write pseudo-code for push and pop operations in an array-based stack implementation.
keyPoints: Push: check overflow, increment top, assign element
Pop: check underflow, store top element, decrement top, return element
Initialize top = -1 for empty stack
Include error handling for overflow/underflow

questions.question: What is the difference between stack overflow and stack underflow? When do they occur?
keyPoints: Overflow: occurs when pushing into a full stack
Underflow: occurs when popping from an empty stack
Overflow happens in fixed-size array implementation
Underflow happens when attempting operations on empty stack

questions.question: Convert the infix expression (A + B) * C - D / E to postfix notation showing all steps.
keyPoints: Start with empty output and operator stack
Process parentheses first: A+B becomes AB+
Then multiplication: (AB+)C* becomes AB+C*
Then division: D/E becomes DE/
Final result: AB+C*DE/-

questions.question: Compare array-based and linked list-based implementations of stack with respect to memory usage and limitations.
keyPoints: Array: fixed size, can cause overflow, may waste memory if underutilized
Linked list: dynamic size, no overflow unless system memory exhausted
Array: O(1) access, simple implementation
Linked list: overhead of pointers, slightly slower due to dynamic allocation

questions.question: Evaluate the postfix expression 10 5 + 3 * 8 - using stack method.
keyPoints: Push 10, push 5
When + encountered: pop 5 and 10, compute 10+5=15, push 15
Push 3, when * encountered: pop 3 and 15, compute 15*3=45, push 45
Push 8, when - encountered: pop 8 and 45, compute 45-8=37
Final result: 37

questions.question: Explain how stack is used in function call recursion with an example.
keyPoints: Each function call pushes return address and local variables onto stack
Recursive calls keep adding to stack until base case reached
Then stack is popped (unwound) returning values
Example: factorial(3) calls factorial(2) calls factorial(1), then returns 2, then 6

questions.question: Write complete C code for implementing a stack using array with all basic operations (push, pop, peek, isEmpty, isFull). Include main function to demonstrate stack operations.
keyPoints: Define MAXSIZE and structure for stack
Initialize top = -1
Implement isFull: returns true if top == MAXSIZE-1
Implement isEmpty: returns true if top == -1
Implement push: check overflow, increment top, assign element
Implement pop: check underflow, decrement top, return element
Implement peek: return top element without modification
Main: demonstrate push, pop, overflow and underflow scenarios

questions.question: Write complete C code for implementing stack using linked list. Explain advantages of this implementation over array implementation.
keyPoints: Define node structure with data and next pointer
Push: create new node, link to current top, update top
Pop: check underflow, store data, move top to next, free memory
Peek: return data at top node
Advantages: dynamic size, no memory wastage, no overflow unless system memory full
Disadvantages: extra memory for pointers, slower due to allocation

questions.question: Explain the algorithm for converting infix expression to postfix using stack. Trace the conversion for: A + B * C - D / (E + F)
keyPoints: Initialize empty output and operator stack
If operand: add to output
If '(' push to stack
If ')' pop until '(' found
If operator: pop higher/equal precedence operators, then push current
At end: pop all remaining operators
Trace: A->AB->ABC*+->ABC*+D->ABC*+DF->ABC*+DFE+->ABC*+DFE+/-

questions.question: Write an algorithm to evaluate postfix expression using stack. Trace it for: 3 4 + 2 * 7 /
keyPoints: Scan left to right
If operand: push to stack
If operator: pop two operands, apply operator, push result
Continue until end of expression
Final stack top is result
Result: 2
